meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding to overcome no portfolio. An intermediate-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: Transform your blank GitHub profile by building a high-conversion user onboarding flow. By implementing feature tours, progress tracking, and interactive tooltips, you demonstrate mastery over user psychology and UI/UX engineering—the exact skills employers crave. This checklist uses Antigravity and Claude Code to turn a standard app into a professional-grade product.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is deafening. You have the code, but you lack the 'wow' factor that signals to recruiters you understand the full lifecycle of a digital product. Most students stop at the login screen, leaving their users stranded. Real-world SaaS success isn't just about features; it's about the first sixty seconds.

Today, we bridge that gap. We are going to engineer a seamless user onboarding experience that turns skeptical visitors into power users. By mastering the art of the 'first-run experience,' you prove you can solve business problems, not just write syntax. Let’s turn your project from a student script into a product-market-fit machine.

steps.title: Architecting the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Define the user journey using a state machine to track onboarding progress, ensuring users never see the same prompt twice.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust state machine for a React-based onboarding flow. Use TypeScript to manage states: 'idle', 'tour_active', 'completed'. Include logic for persistence via localStorage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize state management for onboarding flow in src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts'

steps.title: Implementing Interactive Tooltips
steps.description: Create context-aware tooltips that highlight core features, reducing the cognitive load for new signups.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable Tooltip component using Framer Motion. Ensure it supports Z-index layering and auto-positioning based on target element coordinates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate Tooltip component and integrate with existing dashboard layout'

steps.title: Gamifying the Progress Tracker
steps.description: Add a progress bar that shows users how close they are to 'Value Activation,' increasing completion rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Progress Stepper component that visualizes completion percentage. Include an animation sequence for when a step is marked as finished.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create ProgressStepper component and link to onboarding state'

steps.title: Triggering Contextual Modals
steps.description: Use event-driven triggers to show modals only when the user interacts with specific features, avoiding premature interruptive UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a trigger system that fires an OnboardingModal only after the user clicks the 'New Project' button for the first time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement event listener for project-creation trigger to launch onboarding modal'

steps.title: Analytics and Feedback Loops
steps.description: Track where users drop off in the onboarding flow to identify friction points and prove your data-driven decision-making.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement an analytics tracker that logs event timestamps for each onboarding step completion. Export to a simple JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add telemetry logging to onboarding flow for step-by-step drop-off analysis'

pro_tips: Never force a full-screen tour; allow users to 'Skip' at any point to respect their autonomy.
Use 'Value Activation' metrics to measure success, not just 'Tour Completion' rates.
Implement a 'Help' button that allows users to re-trigger the tour if they get lost later.
Keep your micro-copy conversational and benefit-focused rather than feature-focused.
Always test your onboarding flow on mobile viewports; it is the most common place for UI breakage.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify and update your GitHub README with a high-quality GIF showing the interaction in action. This is now your flagship portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: Stop building invisible apps and start engineering products that retain users from day one. Your portfolio is your resume—make it count. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
